faqs.title: What is the center's holiday schedule?
faqs.answer: Our centers follow a consistent holiday schedule each year, with closures on major federal holidays and a short winter break in late December. A printable holiday schedule is posted on the Parent Resources and FAQs pages by September for the upcoming calendar year. Please note that exact dates, especially in December, may vary slightly year to year, so always refer to the current year calendar or contact the front desk for the most up-to-date schedule.

faqs.title: Which days is the center closed for December holidays?
faqs.answer: Each December we close for a combination of winter holidays and staff training. Typically, the center is closed for at least Christmas Day and either the preceding or following weekday, as well as one to two additional days for winter break. Because specific closure dates change slightly each year, please check the current Holiday Calendar under Parent Resources or contact us directly to receive the full list of December closure dates for this year.

faqs.title: Are tuition payments adjusted for holiday closures?
faqs.answer: Tuition is billed as a flat monthly rate that already accounts for scheduled holiday closures and staff training days. For this reason, we do not prorate tuition for holidays listed on our annual holiday schedule. In the rare case of an unscheduled closure (such as severe weather), credits or make-up options may be considered on a case-by-case basis.

faqs.title: What are your regular hours of operation?
faqs.answer: Our standard hours of operation are Monday through Friday from 7:00 AM to 6:30 PM at most locations. Some programs, including part-day preschool and certain after-school options, may have different start or end times. Please refer to the specific program description or contact your center for exact hours.

faqs.title: What age groups do you serve?
faqs.answer: We currently serve children from 6 weeks through 12 years of age. Our core programs include Infant (6 weeks–18 months), Toddler (18–36 months), Preschool (3–4 years), Pre-K (4–5 years), and After-School (Kindergarten–5th grade). Availability may vary by center location.

faqs.title: What are your typical student-to-teacher ratios?
faqs.answer: Our ratios are designed to meet or exceed New York State licensing standards. Typical maximum ratios are 4:1 for Infants, 5:1 for Young Toddlers, 7:1 for Older Toddlers, 10:1 for Preschool, and 12:1 for School-Age programs. Specific classroom ratios are listed on each classroom detail page.

faqs.title: How do I schedule a tour of the center?
faqs.answer: You can schedule a tour at any time by clicking the "Schedule a Tour" link in the main navigation. Our online tour scheduler allows you to choose a location, date, and time slot that works for your family. You will receive a confirmation email once your request has been submitted.

faqs.title: Is there a waitlist and how do I enroll?
faqs.answer: Some classrooms and age groups may have a waitlist at certain times of the year. To begin enrollment, complete the online "Enroll Now" or "Apply Now" form from your preferred program page. Our team will contact you within 2–3 business days to confirm availability, discuss start dates, and review required paperwork.

faqs.title: How do I use the tuition calculator?
faqs.answer: On the Tuition & Fees page, click "Tuition Calculator" to estimate your monthly cost. Select your child’s age group, enter your ZIP code, and choose the desired schedule (such as 3 days/week or 5 days/week, full-day or part-day). Then click "Calculate" to view an estimated monthly tuition. Estimates are subject to final confirmation by your center director.

faqs.title: Do you offer transportation from local schools for after-school programs?
faqs.answer: Yes, our after-school program currently provides pickup from select local elementary schools, including Lincoln Elementary, Washington Elementary, and a limited number of other nearby schools. Availability may vary by location and route. You can use the school filter on the Programs page to confirm whether we serve your child’s school.

faqs.title: How do you handle food allergies and special diets?
faqs.answer: Families should list all food allergies and dietary needs on the enrollment or tour forms in the "Allergies or Dietary Needs" section. Our kitchen and classroom teams review this information and work together to provide safe meal options, including vegetarian and nut-free choices. We are a peanut- and tree-nut–aware center and do not serve foods with obvious nut ingredients.

faqs.title: Where can I see the current lunch menu?
faqs.answer: You can view the current monthly meal calendar by visiting the "Nutrition & Menu" section of our website. From there, click on a specific date to see the detailed daily menu, including labels for vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and nut-free options.

faqs.title: What is your approach to behavior and guidance?
faqs.answer: We use a positive, relationship-based approach to guidance that focuses on teaching social-emotional skills. Our teachers model respectful communication, help children label their feelings, and use developmentally appropriate strategies such as redirection, problem-solving, and calm-down spaces. We do not use corporal punishment or humiliation under any circumstances.

faqs.title: What is your inclement weather and emergency closure policy?
faqs.answer: In the event of severe weather or other emergencies, we will communicate closures or delayed openings via email, our parent portal, and local news outlets. We do our best to align with local school district decisions while also considering the needs of working families.

faqs.title: How do I register my child for special events?
faqs.answer: Upcoming family and child events are listed on the Events page. Click an event to view details and, if registration is required, complete the quick RSVP form on the event detail page. You will receive a confirmation email once your registration is received.

faqs.title: Can I customize which newsletters and updates I receive?
faqs.answer: Yes. When you subscribe to our newsletter, you can choose your child’s age group and the topics you’re most interested in, such as curriculum updates or events. After subscribing, you can visit the Parent Portal to adjust your email preferences at any time. SMS alerts are optional and can be left turned off if you prefer email only.

faqs.title: Internal test FAQ - not visible to parents
faqs.answer: This FAQ is used internally for testing and should not appear on the public FAQs page.

events.title: Preschool Science Discovery Day
